newsSince: Nichicon notified customers of a broad ~10-15% price hike across its aluminum electrolytic capacitor line (Digitimes, June 17), citing AI-server order volumes exceeding existing capacity plus higher aluminum-foil/energy costs; the move is expected to trigger industry-wide capacitor price increases. This is genuine new fundamental evidence of pricing power and tight supply.

sources.label: Digitimes - Nichicon e-cap price hike / capacity
Gagadget - AI servers drive 15% capacitor price hike

verdicts.rankRationale: New since Jun 9: a 10-15% across-the-board e-cap price hike on AI-server demand exceeding capacity - real pricing-power evidence that reinforces the tight-supply bottleneck leg. But at +154% YTD with sell-side already marking it up, the 'underfollowed' residual-alpha leg keeps eroding; the two roughly offset, so hold.
verdicts.bull: Confirmed pricing power: 10-15% e-cap price hike on AI-server shortage validates the central-node, tight-supply thesis with margin upside into 2H26.
verdicts.neutral: Real fundamental positive, but largely an industry-known dynamic and the stock has tripled; rerating already substantial.
verdicts.bear: At +154% YTD and now sell-side-flagged, the residual-alpha 'hidden' leg is gone; crowding risk dominates on this lens.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Capacitor price hikes flowing through 2H26 margins
verdicts.primaryRisk: Crowding / loss of underfollowed status; raw-material and FX cost inflation
